entries.body: Jordan Maxwell Page not Found! Go Back Home

entries.title: About
entries.body: Hello, My name is Jordan Maxwell! :wave: I’m a professional Game Developer and Web Developer based in Wisconsin. Since 2004 I’ve been living and breathing software development, progressively building and expanding my skillset. Creating everything from video games to distributed networking solutions and AR/VR marketing applications. I’ve had the chance to immerse myself in many different works and projects in the last 17 years, helping to make this world a little better through design & code. Personal Projects and Facts 🔭 I’m currently working on an experimental open source MMO using Panda3D. 🌱 I’m currently learning Kubernetes. ⚡ Fun fact: I’ve been writing software since I was around 8 years old. I was drawn to programming through my interest in video games. :briefcase: Where I am currently working at/as Game Developer at Strange Loop Games :bar_chart: Current Github Stats * Stats only reflect my public repositories 🏆 Trophies Find me elsewhere 🌎 Programming Skills C# 70% Python 90% Cython 80% Java 60% PHP 50% Lua 80% SQL 70% C++ 65% C 50% HTML5/CSS3 90% Visual Basic 50% GLSL 70% Groovy 40% Processing 40% Recent Work History Game Developer, Strange Loop Games, Seattle, Washington 2021 — present In my role as a Software Developer at Strange Loop Games I participated in the ongoing development of our flagship game Eco. I assisted in the development and implementation of new systems and technologies; facilitating and allowing developers and producers to focus on delivering a rich gameplay experience and make informed decisions on future business plans. Some of these contributions include, participating in the focused team for deploying our flagship game internationally and into the Chinese market, developing ElasticSearch based systems for getting live performance statistics from our company infrastructure and game servers as a service offering, and working on cross platform and experimental console support. Web Application Developer & DevOps, Future State Consulting, Green Bay, Wisconsin 2019 — 2021 In my role as a Web Application Developer at Future State Consulting, I successfully designed Vue.js static web applications. I lead the development and implementation of serverless backend systems on top of AWS and Microsoft Azure; facilitating and allowing developers to focus on frontend functionality and UX. Freelance Web Application Developer, M.R. Williams, Inc, United States 2021 — 2021 Software & Data Visualization Developer, Körber Tissue, Green Bay, Wisconsin 2017 — 2020 At Körber Tissue I performed data analysis and visualization and maximized web development productivity by designing custom Python-based applications and services. Additionally, I streamlined legacy VB applications to accomplish specific objectives and perform particular tasks. Also, I am provided outstanding services for the design and development of applications from automated services for business intelligence and augmented/virtual reality projects. Recent Volunteer History Game Developer/Launcher Developer, DarksideRP, Remote 2019 — present - Developing custom gameplay modifications and from scratch systems for Eco. Collaborating with the game's developers on content ideas and QoL improvements. - Developing a custom Vue.js/Electron based launcher for their community and server selection. - Developed custom game modifications for Eco, Red Dead Redemption 2, Grand Theft Auto 5, and ATLAS. - Worked on custom networking solutions for large player capacities outside of normal game limitations. - Developed experimental concepts for administration tools and community engagement. Sound Board Operator, Green Bay Dance Company, Green Bay, Wisconsin 2014 — 2017 Handled the operation of the sound board for theatre performances at St. Norbert College for the annual Spring and Christmas Nutcracker shows. Worked alongside the production crew. http://www.gbdanceco.org/about.html Head of Operations, MineRP, Remote 2014 — 2016 - Managed a team of volunteers to maintain Linux servers and Java applications. - Developed plans for future Java development projects to improve the QoL of the game servers. - Maintained several Java based Minecraft servers and BungeeCord cluster. - Worked with private Maven repositories and Jenkins deployments. - Collaborated weekly with other lead team members on future server project plans.

entries.title: Blog
entries.body: There are currently no posts. Check back later for new content!

entries.title: Games
entries.body: My Games A3P A3P, an online multiplayer third person shooter game written in Panda3D. Available on itch.io! Panda3DPython 3Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: Linux Capture The Packet Capture the Packet was a retro hacker themed multiplayer first person shooter. Built from the ground up using retro raycasting methodologies to generate the 3D level. C++CPlatform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: Linux Eco Eco is a simulation game created by American studio Strange Loop Games, in which players have to work together to create a civilization on a virtual planet. The game values a gentle use of natural resources and is used both as an entertainment and educational tool. Unity3DC#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: Linux Publisher: Strange Loop Games Jölf Jölf is a modding first physics mini golf game made in Unreal Engine 4, Jölf was built originally by Smartly Dressed Games and continued by Digital Descent, LLC. CC++Unreal Engine 4Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: LinuxPlatform: Steam Deck Publisher: Digital Descent, LLC O'ink O’ink is an inventive, cute 3D platformer made in inspiration of the classic 3D platformer titles such as Super Mario N64, Spyro, and the THQ Spongebob series. Unity3DC#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: Linux Publisher: Rumrunner Entertainment, LLC Programmer's Quest! Programmer's Quest! An open source MMO built on top of the Panda3D game engine and Astron server Panda3DPython 3AstronPlayFabPlatform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: LinuxPlatform: Android Safari Rush Unity3DC#PlayFabPlatform: AndroidPlatform: IOS Publisher: Digital Descent, LLC Sketches Through Time Sketches through Time was a prototype point and click adventure game made in Panda3D with artist Anchimayen. You can find more of their artwork on Twitter at @Anchimayen_Art Panda3dPython 3Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOsPlatform: Linux

entries.title: Game Mods
entries.body: My Game Mods Wildstar - Advanced Communicators A Wildstar addon that extends the native CommDisplay addon by Carbine to give it more functionality and player to player communication via the communicators UI LuaPlatform: Windows Game: Wildstar The Sims 3 - Community Server The Sims 3 Online mod allows the creation of 20 player community town servers. Allowing the players to experience a multiplayer live town in real time.This mod is currently in private testing. C#Platform: Windows Game: The Sims 3 Eco - Discord Access Mod This mod provides Discord role based access to Eco servers. Allowing for more automatic control of who can access your community Eco servers. This mod can also be bundled with the native Discord Twitch integration and the Twitch Subscriber role to create subscriber only Eco servers.This mod was originally created to demostrate the usage of the new IUserAuthorizer interface in Eco 9.5+. Any long term support or reliability cannot be guaranteed.Note: This mod is currently dependant on an experimental ModKit api change for Eco 9.5+ C#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOSPlatform: Linux Game: Eco Minecraft - Floating Images The Bukkit Floating Images plugin allows for the creation of floating images by abusing entity packets. Originally created for Mine-RP JavaBukkitPlatform: WindowsPlatform: LinuxPlatform: MacOS Game: Minecraft Eco - Glue Mod The Eco glue mod is a joke meme mod for Eco based around Streamer AdmiralBahroo's glue panda emotes. Based on the actual scientific nutritional values of elmer's glue. The mod exists purely for memes. Has no official affiliation with Eco or gameplay purpose. C#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOSPlatform: Linux Game: Eco Eco - Large Signs Mod An Eco core override mod that extends the Eco signs for large text capacity C#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOSPlatform: Linux Game: Eco Eco - No More Books Mod Tired of players getting a book from the research table just to hand scrolls out to everyone? Want to make your server more challenging? This mod will not allow books to be made on the research table, only scrolls, thus stopping players from making too many scrolls and giving them away. C#Platform: WindowsPlatform: MacOSPlatform: Linux Game: Eco Minecraft - NxtSpeak NxtSpeak is an Bukkit plugin for Minecraft. It creates a TeamSpeak3 in-game interface for your players to interact with your voice server. Originally created for Mine-RP JavaBukkitPlatform: WindowsPlatform: LinuxPlatform: MacOS Game: Minecraft Wildstar - RP Bots An unfinished and abandoned Wildstar addon that allows your bots/pets to join in on Roleplay sessions LuaPlatform: Windows Game: Wildstar

entries.title: Projects
entries.body: My Projects Featured Projects DarksideRP Launcher The DarksideRP launcher is the dedicated portal for accessing all of the DarksideRp.net roleplay and War Games servers. Built using Vue.js and Electron it is able to download, mod, and launch games directly into the game servers while displaying news, rules, and other important information to the users. ElectronVue.jsNode.js Gemstone Framework A full Python 3 data oriented framework for developing dedicated online games, built ontop of the Panda3D module. Panda3DPython 3Qt5 Körber Tissue Virtual Showroom Virtual Showroom was an experimental platform built with Unreal Engine 4 for showcasing equipment and manufacturing machines to customers in a virtual space using Desktop or VR headsets. It was originally created to give a perspective and understanding of equipment at trade shows without the need to ship an entire machine to the location. C++Unreal Engine 4Oculus Quest Pictofeed Pictofeed was an experimental image and video social media platform for Android And IOS. Vue.jsJavascriptPythonAWS Terminal Engine Terminal is a cross platform terminal ASCII art game engine written in C++ with Python bindings through Cython. Intended for making unique ascii based experiences in Python 3 C++CCMakePython Weareable Support Glasses Wearable Glasses was a technological solution developed by Fabio Perini, for remote and real-time technical assistance with 3D interaction using augmented reality provided by the Daqri Headset. entries.title: Toontownremastered
entries.body: Remastered/Archive Edition Toontown Online, commonly known as Toontown, was a 3D massively multiplayer online role-playing game based on a cartoon animal world, developed by Disney’s Virtual Reality Studio and Schell Games, and published by The Walt Disney Company. The game made its official launch on June 2, 2003. The game closed September 19th, 2013 due to shifts in the company’s management and budget citing the industrys change away from subscription based games. Screenshots Legal Notice This archival project is not affiliated with The Walt Disney Company and/or the Disney Interactive Media Group. It exists purely as a personal project and means of helping preserve an MMO that defined the childrens MMO genre.

entries.title: Toontownvr
entries.body: Virtual Reality Edition Toontown VR was an experimental one off port of Disney’s Toontown designed to run under Valve’s OpenVR library. The user interface was not ported and controls were incomplete resulting in the user needing to take the headset off regularly to interact with the keyboard/mouse. To achieve the required performance to run the 20+ year old game under virtual reality the codebase had to be upgraded to the latest version of Panda3D and the Python 3 language. A custom render window was also written in later iterations to render directly to OpenVR from within the C++ engine code further reducing the load Python had to handle for optimal performance. Live Stream Demo On October 25th, 2020 a live demo was recorded of an early version of the prototype. The demo was recored with an Oculus Rift S and had frame rate issues because at the time the OpenVR composit submit was happening in the single threaded Python side of the Panda3D engine. Resulting in a lot of global interpretor lock interaction going back and forth from C++ to Python The live stream archive can be found here.
